text:
**Layer-certification registry — formal architectural-layer enumeration with per-layer pass/fail gate**

## Problem

Per tele-8 (Gated Recursive Integrity) Mandate: "If it cannot be proven under chaos in a sandboxed environment, it does not exist in production." The Sovereign Onion principle requires each layer be bit-perfect and physically sealed before the next layer ascends. Today the architecture's layers are documented prose-only (e.g., L0 Hub protocol, L1 entity-registry, L2 policy-router, L3 threads+cascade, L4 cognitive-layer, L5 adapter) without:
- a structured registry naming each layer,
- a per-layer pass/fail certification gate,
- a known-good commit pinned at the certification moment,
- a discoverable traversal that lets reviewers verify "Layer N+1 cannot deploy unless Layer N is green."

Closes the **Foundation-of-Sand fault** (tele-8): "high abstractions built on unverified assumptions" — and the **Surface Patching fault**: "symptoms addressed without audit of the failing layer."

## Proposed scope (sketch)

A `layer-certification-registry` artifact (Hub entity OR canonical document) carrying:

```
layers: [
  { id: "L0", name: "Hub MCP Protocol",     certifiedAt: <ts>, certifiedAtCommit: <sha>, gateStatus: "green" | "yellow" | "red", nextGateCriterion: <text> },
  { id: "L1", name: "Entity Registry SSOT", certifiedAt: ..., gateStatus: ..., dependsOn: ["L0"] },
  { id: "L2", name: "Policy Router",        certifiedAt: ..., dependsOn: ["L1"] },
  { id: "L3", name: "Threads 2.0 + Cascade",dependsOn: ["L2"] },
  { id: "L4", name: "Cognitive Layer",      dependsOn: ["L3"] },
  { id: "L5", name: "Adapter (engineer)",   dependsOn: ["L4"] },
]
```

Each layer's gate criteria explicit and machine-verifiable.

## Cross-references

- **tele-8** (Gated Recursive Integrity) — primary alignment
- **2026-04 architectural review Phase 1 cartography** — tele-8 reverse-gap closure
- **Architect critique** (`docs/reviews/2026-04-phase-1-cartography-critique.md` §4.2.1) — author of this proposal: lily (architect, eng-40903c59d19f), filed by greg (engineer) during Pass 1.1 revision
- **bug-15** (INV-TH17 turn-pinning observability gap) — related to per-layer instrumentation
- **idea-128** (gitops multi-engineer concurrent work) — adjacent (cross-engineer coordination, different layer)

## Status

Reverse-gap closure seed — needs architect-led design + brief before any execution.
